query: ECB interest rate decision May 2026, euro short-term rate €STR current level and upcoming changes
answer:
Now I have all the data I need. Let me compile the comprehensive research report.

---

# ECB Interest Rate Decision May 2026 & €STR Current Level — Research Report

## 1. Current ECB Key Interest Rates (as of 4 May 2026)

Based on verified data from the ECB's Statistical Data Warehouse API (data-api.ecb.europa.eu), the three key ECB interest rates are:

| Rate | Current Level | Since |
|---|---|---|
| **Deposit Facility Rate (DFR)** | **2.00%** | 11 June 2025 |
| **Main Refinancing Operations (MRO)** | **2.15%** | 11 June 2025 |
| **Marginal Lending Facility** | **2.40%** | 11 June 2025 |

The spread between the DFR and MRO is 15 bps, and the spread between MRO and marginal lending facility is 25 bps — consistent with the ECB's revised operational framework adopted in 2024.

## 2. Full ECB Rate-Cutting Cycle (June 2024 – Present)

The ECB undertook a sustained easing cycle from June 2024 through June 2025, cutting the deposit facility rate by **200 basis points** across **seven consecutive cuts**:

| Effective Date | Deposit Facility Rate | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 12 June 2024 | 3.75% | −25 bps |
| 18 September 2024 | 3.50% | −25 bps |
| 23 October 2024 | 3.25% | −25 bps |
| 18 December 2024 | 3.00% | −25 bps |
| 5 February 2025 | 2.75% | −25 bps |
| 12 March 2025 | 2.50% | −25 bps |
| 23 April 2025 | 2.25% | −25 bps |
| **11 June 2025** | **2.00%** | **−25 bps** |

**Rates have been unchanged since June 2025** — a pause of nearly 11 months as of today.

## 3. Latest ECB Decision: 30 April 2026 — RATES HELD UNCHANGED

On **30 April 2026**, the Governing Council decided **unanimously to keep all three key ECB interest rates unchanged**. This was confirmed in the press conference by President Christine Lagarde.

**Key highlights from the April 30, 2026 monetary policy statement:**

- **Rates held due to conflicting forces**: Upside risks to inflation and downside risks to growth have both intensified simultaneously.
- **War in the Middle East**: A sharp increase in energy prices caused by the war is pushing inflation up while weighing on economic activity.
- **Inflation rose to 3.0% in April** (from 2.6% in March, 1.9% in February). Energy inflation surged to 10.9%. Core inflation (ex-energy, ex-food) fell to 2.2%.
- **GDP growth was 0.1% in Q1 2026**. Unemployment remained at 6.2% (near historical lows).
- **No second-round effects observed yet**, but the ECB is closely monitoring propagation.
- Lagarde confirmed that **both holding and hiking were debated** at length; the decision to hold was unanimous.
- Lagarde indicated the ECB's **March 2026 projections** had embedded **two rate hikes** in both the baseline and scenario analyses.
- The ECB described itself as "well-positioned" given inflation was near 2% when the energy shock hit, and stated it would take a **"data-dependent, meeting-by-meeting approach."**
- **June projections and updated scenarios** will be critical for the next decision.

## 4. Euro Short-Term Rate (€STR) — Current Level

From the ECB's data API, the latest €STR fixings:

| Date | €STR Rate (%) |
|---|---|
| 30 April 2026 | **1.930** |
| 29 April 2026 | 1.932 |
| 28 April 2026 | 1.932 |
| 27 April 2026 | 1.932 |
| 24 April 2026 | 1.933 |
| 23 April 2026 | 1.933 |
| 22 April 2026 | 1.931 |
| 21 April 2026 | 1.932 |
| 20 April 2026 | 1.931 |
| 17 April 2026 | 1.932 |

**€STR is currently at ~1.93%**, approximately **7 basis points below the deposit facility rate** of 2.00%. This is a typical spread for €STR relative to the DFR.

**€STR transition history** (tracking the easing cycle via €STR fixings):

| Date | €STR Rate | Event |
|---|---|---|
| 3 June 2024 | 3.913% | Pre-first cut |
| 12 June 2024 | 3.662% | After 1st cut |
| 18 September 2024 | 3.416% | After 2nd cut |
| 23 October 2024 | 3.166% | After 3rd cut |
| 18 December 2024 | 2.917% | After 4th cut |
| 5 February 2025 | 2.666% | After 5th cut |
| 12 March 2025 | 2.412% | After 6th cut |
| 23 April 2025 | 2.167% | After 7th cut |
| **11 June 2025** | **1.924%** | **After 8th (final) cut** |

€STR has been stable around 1.93% since June 2025, consistent with the DFR hold at 2.00%.

**Volume concentration**: The share of the 5 largest active banks in €STR volume was 41–48% in late April 2026.

## 5. Upcoming ECB Monetary Policy Meetings (2026)

From the official ECB Governing Council calendar:

| Date (Decision Day) | Type |
|---|---|
| **11 June 2026** | Monetary policy meeting — press conference |
| **23 July 2026** | Monetary policy meeting — press conference |
| **10 September 2026** | Monetary policy meeting (Bundesbank) — press conference |
| **29 October 2026** | Monetary policy meeting — press conference |
| **17 December 2026** | Monetary policy meeting — press conference |

**The next rate decision is 11 June 2026** — exactly six weeks from the April 30 meeting. This meeting will include new staff macroeconomic projections and updated scenarios, which Lagarde specifically flagged as critical.

## 6. Context for Upcoming Changes

- The April 30 statement and Q&A strongly suggest the ECB is **leaning toward a rate hike in June**, but is waiting for more data on energy price propagation and second-round effects.
- Markets had been pricing in a rate hike for June, and Lagarde did not push back against this.
- The March 2026 staff projections had **two rate hikes embedded** in both the baseline and scenarios.
- The key variables the ECB is watching: duration of the Middle East conflict, energy prices, wage negotiations, selling price expectations, supply chain disruptions.
- Growth projection: 0.9% for 2026, 1.3% for 2027, 1.4% for 2028 (March baseline).

key_facts: ECB Governing Council held all three key interest rates unchanged on 30 April 2026 (unanimous decision)
ECB deposit facility rate stands at 2.00% since 11 June 2025, main refinancing rate at 2.15%, marginal lending facility at 2.40%
The ECB cut rates 8 times between June 2024 and June 2025, reducing the deposit facility rate by 200 bps (from 4.00% to 2.00%)
Rates have been on hold for nearly 11 months since the last cut in June 2025
Euro area inflation rose to 3.0% in April 2026 (from 2.6% March, 1.9% February), driven by energy prices surging due to war in Middle East
Energy inflation reached 10.9% in April 2026; core inflation (ex-energy, ex-food) fell to 2.2%
€STR (euro short-term rate) is at 1.930% as of 30 April 2026, approximately 7 bps below the deposit facility rate
€STR has been stable around 1.93% since June 2025, consistent with the unchanged DFR
Next ECB monetary policy decision is 11 June 2026, with new staff projections and updated scenarios
Lagarde confirmed both holding and hiking were debated at the April meeting; March projections had two rate hikes embedded in baseline
Euro area GDP grew 0.1% in Q1 2026; unemployment at 6.2% (near historical lows)
ECB described risks as downside for growth, upside for inflation; 'tilted' language removed
No second-round effects from energy price shock observed yet, but ECB closely monitoring propagation
Remaining 2026 monetary policy meetings: 11 June, 23 July, 10 September, 29 October, 17 December
